note: Second research session — topics deliberately chosen in gaps not yet covered by the existing ~130 posts (testing, ORM, passkeys, tooling, realtime, observability, WebGPU, roles).

topics.title: Testing Next.js 16 for Real: Vitest, Playwright, and Server Actions
topics.whyTrending: The blog has deep Next.js coverage but almost nothing on testing. RSC + Server Actions broke old testing assumptions and there's high search intent for how to unit-test actions and E2E-test App Router flows in 2026.

topics.title: Drizzle vs Prisma in 2026: The Honest TypeScript ORM Decision
topics.whyTrending: ORM choice is a constant debate and completely uncovered on the blog; Drizzle's edge/serverless story vs Prisma's DX split teams. High practical + SEO value.

topics.title: Passkeys in Next.js: Killing the Password with WebAuthn
topics.whyTrending: Passkeys hit mainstream adoption in 2025-2026; ties directly to the new auth pillar (NextAuth/Clerk) but the WebAuthn implementation itself is uncovered and highly searched.

topics.title: Biome 2.0: Replacing ESLint + Prettier With One Rust Tool
topics.whyTrending: Biome (56x faster linting) is the Rust-toolchain story of 2026; the blog has a strict-ESLint guide, so a migration-to-Biome piece is a natural, timely counterpart.

topics.title: Client State in the RSC Era: Do You Still Need Zustand?
topics.whyTrending: Server Components shrank the role of client state, but nobody clearly maps what still belongs in Zustand/Jotai vs the server. The blog covers XState and prop-drilling but not this reframing.

topics.title: SSE vs WebSockets in Next.js: Choosing Your Real-Time Transport
topics.whyTrending: Real-time is a known Next.js weak spot (server actions can't do it); a practical SSE-vs-WebSocket guide for App Router fills a real gap and pairs with the AI streaming article.

topics.title: Observability for Next.js: OpenTelemetry, Traces, and the Serverless Blind Spot
topics.whyTrending: The React2Shell post-mortem highlighted that you can't image a serverless function — observability is the answer and it's uncovered. OpenTelemetry + Next.js instrumentation has strong ops search intent.

topics.title: The Design Engineer: The Role Quietly Reshaping Frontend Teams
topics.whyTrending: Career/opinion content performs well on the blog (team-lead, fractional-CTO pieces); the design-engineer role is a hot 2026 topic with little rigorous writing and strong shareability.

topics.title: WebGPU in Practice: GPU Compute in the Browser Without a PhD
topics.whyTrending: WebGPU shipped broadly and is the successor to WebGL; the blog has a WebAssembly piece but nothing on WebGPU. Cutting-edge, demo-able, low competition.

topics.title: Transactional Email as React Components: React Email + Resend
topics.whyTrending: React Email + Resend became the default 2026 stack for developer-friendly transactional email; entirely uncovered on the blog, highly practical, strong SEO for a common real-world task.
